summary: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service that handles authentication, authorization, and policy enforcement for users, devices, apps, and resources. It is the foundational identity layer used automatically by all Microsoft 365, Azure, and Dynamics CRM Online tenants. The broader Microsoft Entra family extends this with governance, protection, external access, and workload identity capabilities.

key_concepts.explanation: Cloud-based identity provider that authenticates users and enforces access policies; every Azure subscription comes with a tenant automatically.
Managed domain service providing legacy protocols (LDAP, Kerberos, NTLM, Group Policy) for apps that cannot use modern authentication — Microsoft runs the domain controllers for you.
Detects risky sign-ins and compromised identities, and can automatically enforce remediation via risk-based Conditional Access policies.
Policy engine that evaluates conditions (user, location, device, risk level) at sign-in time and enforces controls such as MFA or block.
Automates access lifecycle: provisioning, access reviews, and deprovisioning based on identity lifecycle events like joining or leaving the company.
Manages identities for guests, partners, and consumers (B2B and CIAM), supporting social logins and self-service registration.
Identity solution for non-human identities such as apps, services, and containers that need to authenticate to Azure resources.
Security model that verifies every access request explicitly rather than trusting by network location; Microsoft Entra is the control plane for Zero Trust.
A dedicated instance of Microsoft Entra ID for an organization, automatically created with an onmicrosoft.com domain; custom domains can be added.
REST API used to programmatically manage Entra ID resources such as users, groups, and licenses.

exam_focus_points: Every Azure subscription is automatically associated with one Microsoft Entra ID tenant.
Microsoft Entra Domain Services provides managed Kerberos/NTLM/LDAP — it does NOT sync passwords back to on-premises AD.
Conditional Access policies are evaluated at sign-in and can require MFA, compliant device, or block access based on risk level.
Difference between Microsoft Entra ID Free, P1, and P2 license tiers and which features (e.g., ID Protection, Governance) require P2.
Microsoft Entra ID Governance handles joiner/mover/leaver lifecycle automation and access reviews.
Microsoft Entra External ID covers both B2B (guest collaboration) and B2C/CIAM (customer-facing apps) scenarios.
Custom domain names can be added to a tenant and must be verified via DNS record.
Microsoft Graph API is the programmatic interface for managing Entra ID resources and automating admin tasks.
Microsoft Entra Workload ID is used to assign managed identities to apps, services, and containers.
similar_concepts: Active Directory Domain Services (AD DS) — on-premises Windows Server AD
Microsoft Entra Domain Services — managed legacy domain service, often confused with Entra ID itself
Microsoft Entra ID Governance vs. Microsoft Entra ID Protection — governance is about lifecycle/access reviews; protection is about risk detection
Microsoft Entra External ID vs. Entra ID — External ID is for guests/customers, not internal employees
common_mistakes: Confusing Microsoft Entra ID (cloud IdP) with on-premises Active Directory Domain Services — they are different products with different protocols.
Thinking Microsoft Entra Domain Services is the same as Entra ID — Domain Services is a separate managed service for legacy LDAP/Kerberos workloads.
Assuming Conditional Access is available on the Free tier — advanced Conditional Access policies require P1 or higher.
Believing you must create a new tenant when signing up for Azure — a tenant is automatically provisioned.
Confusing B2B (guest collaboration via External ID) with B2C/CIAM (customer identity management) — they have different configuration paths and use cases.
Thinking ID Protection automatically blocks risky users without configuration — an administrator must create and enable risk-based Conditional Access policies.
